ResourceDictionary.x:String: SharePoint content in Windows File Explorer.
Pin a SharePoint folder to see it here and in File Explorer.
Pin a SharePoint folder to make it appear in File Explorer.
Files waiting to sync with SharePoint.
Go to the folder you want to use and pin it in EasyShare.
To keep your session active, select "Yes" when asked to stay signed in.
If IT did not provide a Client ID, use Sign in through the app.
Use these fields only when IT provides the application details.
Choose how EasyShare looks. System is the default and follows Windows.
The change is applied immediately and saved when you confirm the settings.
The color used for highlighted elements in the app.
Increases the difference between the background, text, and action elements.
Resets your EasyShare settings. No SharePoint files are deleted.
Review language, access, Windows integration, cache, and notifications in a guided flow.
Quick answers for setting up and using EasyShare every day.
Which access mode should I use?
Use Sign in through the app when you only have your Microsoft account. Use Company Client ID only when IT provides it.
Why should I choose Stay signed in?
It helps EasyShare continue accessing SharePoint without asking you to sign in repeatedly.
How do I pin a folder?
Open Session, sign in to SharePoint, go to the folder you want, and select Pin current folder.
The drive did not appear. What should I check?
Make sure WinFsp is installed, at least one folder is pinned, and Create drive automatically is enabled in Settings.
How do I change a pinned folder?
Open Folders and select Edit next to the folder. You can change its name or link.
Why does the app stay open after I close it?
EasyShare stays in the Windows notification area to keep the drive active. To fully exit, use Exit from the tray icon.
When should I reset the app?
Reset only when you want to start over on this computer. It clears local settings but does not delete SharePoint files.
EasyShare · simple access to your work folders
Your folders stay organized in one place.
Sign in using the simplest flow for your environment.
Check for new versions directly from the app.
EasyShare automatically checks GitHub Releases for new versions.
This installation receives updates directly from Microsoft Store.
Checking for available updates in Microsoft Store.
Version {0} is available: {1}. Download it to update.
Version {0} is available in Microsoft Store. Confirm to download and install it through the Store.
This release did not publish a changelog. Review the release details before installing.
A required EasyShare update is available.
Microsoft Store reports that version {0} is up to date.
The GitHub update response could not be understood.
The update repository was not configured in this build.
There is no public release at {0} yet.
The release tag ({0}) does not look like a valid version.
Version {0} exists, but it did not publish a patch compatible with this installation. The app never downloads a full installer automatically.
Release {0} did not publish the installer's SHA-256 digest. The download was blocked for your protection.
Could not query releases at {0}. Check your connection and make sure the repository is public.
Could not query Microsoft Store. Check your connection and try again later.
Downloading only the patch for version {0}.
Download complete. Open the installer; it may request administrator permission to finish the update.
Could not download the installer. Try again later.
Windows will request your confirmation, then download and install the update through Microsoft Store.
Microsoft Store completed the update. Reopen EasyShare if Windows closes the app.
The Microsoft Store update was canceled. You can try again whenever you want.
Microsoft Store could not complete the update. Try again later.
Microsoft Store did not complete the update. State: {0}.
Confirm administrator permission if Windows asks, finish the installation, and reopen EasyShare.
The installer could not be opened or permission was canceled.
File downloaded and saved: {0}. Ready to install.
Applying the changes on this computer.
Clearing local data from this computer.
EasyShare could not read the settings. Restart the app or contact IT.
The information is up to date.
The folder is removed from EasyShare. Nothing is deleted from SharePoint.
Open the folder you want to pin and try again.
Sign-in removed. Sign in again to access SharePoint.
Sign in, choose Stay signed in, and open the folder you want to pin.
Then open the folder and select Pin current folder.
How do you want to access SharePoint?
Use your account in EasyShare's built-in browser. This is the simplest option.
Use this if IT provided an Application (client) ID.
If you already have the folder link, paste it here.
On the Microsoft page, choose Stay signed in when the option appears.
Enter a SharePoint address, such as mycompany.sharepoint.com.
Use this only if IT has already provided these details.
Paste the Application (client) ID provided by IT.
Open the folder in SharePoint, copy the browser address, and paste it here.
EasyShare automatically identifies the site and path.
Paste a SharePoint link, such as https://company.sharepoint.com/sites/team.
This name appears as a folder inside the EasyShare drive.
It is already available on the drive.
This deletes settings, saved sign-in data, pinned folders, and the upload queue from this computer. Nothing is deleted from SharePoint.
The app returned to first-time setup.
Choose how to sign in to SharePoint.
Sign in from the Session page.
Open the Session page to sign in.
Sign in through the app when you need to renew access.
Windows did not allow startup activation. Enable it under Settings > Apps > Startup.
Ready to sign in with Microsoft.
IT must provide the Application (client) ID for this mode.
Sign in to access your folders.
Account connected on this Windows device.
Administrator approval has not been completed for this application.
The Entra Redirect URI must be http://localhost.
Sign-in was canceled or blocked by Microsoft.
Sign in on the Session page before testing.
You are not signed in to this SharePoint yet.
You do not have permission for this site.
Sign-in ready. Pin a folder to get started.
Temporary caches were removed without deleting the saved sign-in.
Sign in with your account before testing.
You do not have permission for this folder.
Site, library, or folder not found.
Enable it in Settings to open it in File Explorer.
Install WinFsp to create the drive.
Pin a folder to create the drive.
WinFsp rejected the mount point. NTSTATUS code 0x{0:X8}.
Run the check to refresh EasyShare health.
The local database did not respond.
No pinned folder requires a session.
Valid cookies are available for the routes.
Open the session to complete this check.
Integration is unavailable; in-app notices remain active.
{0} file(s) are available in encrypted form.
The offline cache could not be checked.
A file needs your decision in EasyShare.
An upload could not finish. Open Operations Center.
The pending local copy will be discarded. Export it first if you want to preserve it.
This can overwrite another person's change and requires explicit confirmation.
The decision was applied to the sync queue.
The pending content was exported securely.
Choose privacy-safe alerts and limit encrypted storage for use without a network.
Some options are set by IT and cannot be changed on this device.
Open EasyShare to renew the corporate session.
The virtual drive needs attention in Health Center.
A validated update is ready to install.
The safe support package was saved.
The selected content was stored in encrypted form.
The offline copy was removed without deleting SharePoint content.
I understand this can replace another person's remote change.
The ZIP will include version, Windows, architecture, and redacted logs. Tokens, cookies, the database, offline cache, and file contents are excluded.
The SharePoint host is not on the IT allowlist.
Interactive sign-in was disabled by your organization.
Find sites your account follows or can search, browse libraries, and pin a folder without copying URLs.
Microsoft Graph honors your permissions, but this list is not a complete tenant inventory. Search by name when a site does not appear; manual URL entry remains available.
Check your connection and permissions, then try again.
Use the corporate account configured by the client_id to see only destinations you can access.
Sign in or refresh to discover your SharePoint sites.
No sites were found. Search by name or enter a URL manually.
This site has no libraries available to your account.
{0} is now available in EasyShare folders.
This destination is already available in EasyShare.
Choose a site, a library, and the folder you want to pin.
This route destination is protected so pending uploads cannot be redirected. To change folders, pin a new route and remove the old one after its queue is complete.
SharePoint validation took too long. Check the connection and try again.
Your account does not have permission for this resource. Choose another site or request access from its owner.
Nothing was changed. The wizard will appear again on the next launch and is also available in Settings.
Your preferences were saved. You can review them at any time in Settings.
Setup was saved, but the connection could not be opened now. Use Sign in or open Explorer when you are ready.
Review the indicated fields and try again. No incomplete setup was applied.
Preferences were saved, but a Windows integration could not be updated now. Review its status in Settings.
EasyShare is completing another operation. Wait and try again.
Choose Microsoft Graph or the integrated browser session.
Microsoft Graph access was set by your organization and cannot be overridden.
The integrated browser session has been disabled by your organization.
Enter a valid Client ID supplied by your organization.
Enter organizations, a domain, or a valid tenant identifier.
This tenant is not on your organization's allowed list.
Enter a valid HTTPS SharePoint URL or keep the Microsoft 365 portal.
Choose a drive letter from D: through Z:.
The drive letter set by your organization is already in use. Contact your IT team.
This drive letter is already in use. Choose another one.
Cache duration must be between 1 and 1,440 minutes.
Offline storage must be between 128 MB and 102,400 MB.
A simple guide to prepare access, Windows, and storage for you.
Choose how EasyShare communicates with you.
Adjust theme and contrast for comfortable reading.
Choose how to connect to SharePoint.
Enter only the information that is needed.
Configure the virtual drive and startup.
Let's prepare EasyShare with choices explained in plain language. You can change everything later in Settings.
Each option explains what changes and which setup usually works best.
EasyShare never asks for a client secret and respects your account permissions.
Cancel without saving, connect later, or run this wizard again whenever you want.
Use the Windows theme for a familiar experience or choose a fixed appearance.
Increases visual separation between text, surfaces, and controls.
Language, theme, and contrast change while you choose. Canceling restores the previous appearance.
Microsoft Graph is the best option because a Client ID is available or an organization policy requires this access.
The integrated session is the simplest option on this computer. If IT supplies a Client ID, you can use SharePoint Explorer.
Discovers sites and libraries allowed for your account and lets you pin folders without finding URLs. Requires a Client ID from your organization.
Opens SharePoint inside EasyShare and reuses your session. It is useful when Microsoft Graph has not been configured yet.
Enter the public details of the application registered by your organization. No client secret is needed.
Sign-in happens in the official Microsoft window after you finish. EasyShare stores only the protected cache needed to maintain the session.
Use the Microsoft 365 portal or enter the HTTPS address of the SharePoint site you access frequently.
Only HTTPS Microsoft 365 and SharePoint addresses are accepted. External addresses or embedded credentials are blocked.
Performs light checks while EasyShare is open. Use this only on a trusted computer.
The virtual drive shows your pinned folders in File Explorer. It requires WinFsp to be installed.
You can still use EasyShare normally. Install WinFsp later to enable the virtual drive.
Recommended when WinFsp is available. Only free drive letters are offered.
S: is preferred when available. If it is already in use, choose another available letter.
Starts EasyShare after you sign in to Windows to keep uploads and the drive available.
Opens quietly in the background. This option works only with automatic startup.
The recommended values work well for most people and can be adjusted later.
15 minutes reduces requests without keeping old information for too long.
2,048 MB is a balanced starting point. EasyShare respects this local limit.
Keeps notifications configured but avoids showing them while quiet mode is active.
Check the summary. When you finish, all valid choices are saved together.
Organization policies are checked again. If writing fails, your previous settings remain active.
Connect and choose my first folder now
After saving, EasyShare opens sign-in and takes you to SharePoint Explorer or the integrated session.
Setup could not be saved now. Your previous settings remain active; try again.
